reasoning: A short-term 1-week forecast for CCC effective yields is heavily influenced by short-term momentum and mean reversion. The yield recently spiked significantly to 13.61 on Friday, March 13, 2026, breaking sharply above its recent (upward) trend line. Given the sharp run-up, standard time series trend models predict a partial reversion to the trend line (approximately 13.52 by March 22), estimating a roughly 40% probability of a further increase over the next week. Yields exhibit mean-reverting behavior after sharp short-term moves, suggesting the probability of continuing to rise over the exact 1-week horizon is slightly below 50%. Based on the FRED statistical analysis and the recent steep spike, I estimate a 39% chance that it increases further between March 15 and March 22.
